summary: Soundboard capture of a tight, high‑energy early‑80s Greek show – scorching first set and standout versions of Jack Straw, Lazy Lightning > Supplication, China–Rider and Not Fade Away.
review: The recording is a clean soundboard source that delivers most of the band’s detail with only occasional tape‑hiss and level fluctuation. Musically the concert shines – the first set smokes from Jack Straw through Deal, while the second set offers a fierce China → Rider jam, a ripping Lazy Lightning > Supplication pair and a funky Not Fade Away. Overall fans praise Jerry’s vocals, Brent’s soulful Good Times Blues and the tight rhythm section.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive fan feedback (many 5‑star reviews) highlights a strong performance and a clear soundboard recording; only minor complaints about tape hiss or occasional song slips keep the rating just below perfection.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with good instrument separation. Reviewers note some tape‑hiss and occasional level fluctuations, but overall the audio is very listenable.

poor_songs: Lazy Lightning (some reviewers felt it lacked bite)
show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑energy first set that “smokes” from start to finish, followed by a tight, faster‑paced second set with strong jams and a seamless flow into the encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals described as strong, clear and among his best; guitar tone praised on several tracks.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mix gives Phil’s bass a prominent, clean presence – many note “good Phil” in the soundboard.
band_member_comments.Bob: Solid vocal performance throughout; highlighted for penny‑whistle work during Space and overall form.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s Good Times Blues (aka Never Trust a Woman) singled out with praise for his vocal reverb and soulful delivery.
band_member_comments.Drums: Tight, confident drumming noted; rhythm section described as “superbly tight”.
